meta.title: AI Implementation Plan Error Prevention Guide
meta.source_pdf: AI Implementation Plan Error Prevention Guide.pdf
notes: This JSON is a machine-consumable derivative generated via text extraction. Preserve the canonical PDF/MD for human review.
Rule text is preserved as directive lines; code examples are separated when detected.

mandatory_pre_execution_gates.name: Structural Integrity Check
mandatory_pre_execution_gates.timing: Run BEFORE any other planning work begins.

required_validations.heading: No contradictory definitions - Search entire plan for:
details: Same concept defined multiple ways (e.g., “file_id is 20 digits” vs “file_id is 22
Conflicting schema constraints (required: true AND required: false for same
Multiple incompatible ID formats for same entity type

required_validations.heading: All structural elements present:
details: All declared validation gates have definitions
All referenced schemas have corresponding files
All data structures have complete required fields (no empty placeholders like

required_validations.heading: Single canonical ID scheme:
details: ONE format per entity type (not “UUID-based OR 1000-series”)
All existing references use the canonical format
Migration plan provided if legacy IDs exist
NO mixing of 16-digit, 19-digit, and 20-digit IDs for same concept
ABORT if: Any contradiction, missing element, or ID scheme conflict detected.

mandatory_pre_execution_gates.abort_if: Any contradiction, missing element, or ID scheme conflict detected.

rules.label: A
rules.name: Timestamp Contamination
directives: Content hashes used for change detection
Sorting keys that affect output ordering
examples: Every hashing/ID generation operation MUST specify:
No floating point (use string decimals or integers)
ASCII encoding policy: ensure_ascii=True or explicit UTF-8
Whitespace policy: no pretty-print for hashes
Define semantic sort key (e.g., relative_path , geu_id , timestamp )
Sort before serialization: sorted(items, key=lambda x: x['semantic_key'])
ABORT if: Any ID/hash generation lacks explicit canonicalization protocol.

rules.label: B
rules.name: No Leading-Zero Loss
examples: 3. Database schema: Use VARCHAR(20), not BIGINT
Validation: Scan all ID fields in output for:
Any ID that changed length during processing

rules.label: A
rules.name: No Labels in ID Fields
directives: NEVER allow human-readable labels where canonical IDs required:
examples: Validation rule: All fields ending in _id , _geu_id , _file_id must contain exactly 20

rules.label: B
rules.name: Type-Code Semantic Correctness
directives: ID prefixes/ranges must match entity type:
examples: Implementation: Define and enforce ID allocation ranges in schema:
raise ValueError(f"ID {id_value} not in range for {expected_type}")

rules.label: A
rules.name: Type Stability
directives: Field types MUST NOT vary across records:
examples: Validation: Type-check all records for each field:

rules.label: B
rules.name: Empty Container Semantics
examples: NEVER mix: [] vs null without documented semantic difference.

rules.label: B
rules.name: Snapshot Pinning
directives: For all decisions based on external state:
examples: # Now safe to use current_snapshot for decisions
Require: Every plan that reads external state must:
2. Validate no drift before applying changes
3. Update baseline only after successful commit

rules.label: A
rules.name: No Manual Edits to Derived Fields
directives: Mark all derived fields as read-only:
examples: if any(field in changes for field in DERIVED_FIELDS):
raise ValueError(f"Cannot manually edit derived fields: {DERIVED_FIELDS}")

rules.label: B
rules.name: Rebuild After Base Change
examples: Validation: Check for stale derived data:
raise DerivedDataStale(f"file {file['file_id']} geu_ids out of sync")

rules.label: A
rules.name: Deterministic Merge Policy
examples: return {**source1, **source2} # Last-write-wins, but which is last?

rules.label: B
rules.name: Duplication Classification
directives: 1. Join duplication (expected): Same entity appears in multiple joins
Example: File in 3 GEUs → 3 entries, same file_id
2. True duplication (data quality issue): Same logical entity, multiple IDs
Example: Same file path, two file_ids
3. Corruption duplication (integrity error): ID collision, different content

rules.label: B
rules.name: Reconciliation States
directives: 'SUBSET': 'Planned files are subset of observed (extras exist)',
'SUPERSET': 'Planned files are superset of observed (missing files)',
'OVERLAP': 'Partial match (some planned, some observed, some both)',
examples: planned_ids = set(f['file_id'] for f in planned_files)
observed_ids = set(f['file_id'] for f in observed_files)

rules.label: A
rules.name: CAS (Compare-And-Swap) Baseline
examples: raise LockTimeout(f"Could not acquire lock in {timeout_seconds}s")

rules.label: B
rules.name: Spec-Implementation Alignment
directives: CLI behavior must match documented specification:
examples: Validation: Parse CLI help text and compare to spec
assert arg_name in actual_args, f"Missing argument: {arg_name}"

rules.label: C
rules.name: Mode Selection Clarity
examples: help='UNSAFE: Allow validation warnings (use only for development)'
"PERMISSIVE MODE ENABLED. Results not suitable for production.\n"
"Re-run with default strict mode for governance-grade validation."

rules.label: A
rules.name: Post-Apply Evidence
directives: Every mutation must produce PASS evidence:

rules.label: C
rules.name: Gate Completeness
directives: Validation gates must check evidence bundle completeness:
examples: f"Cannot mark gate as PASS without complete evidence bundle."

rules.label: A
rules.name: Registry-Policy Separation
directives: Registry = descriptive truth, NOT procedural rules:
examples: // Rules go in separate policy document

rules.label: B
rules.name: No AI Heuristics as SSOT
directives: AI-inferred values must be validated before becoming truth:
examples: # WRONG - AI suggestion directly to SSOT

rules.label: C
rules.name: Variance Management Outside Registry
examples: # WRONG - variance rules stuffed into registry

body_lines: All validation gates have definitions (not just declarations)
All referenced schemas exist as files (not just mentioned)
All data structures have required fields populated (no empty placeholders like {} )
All cross-references resolve (e.g., file_id in entries[] matches file in files[])
No contradictory definitions (same concept defined multiple ways)
Single canonical ID scheme (no mixed 16/19/20-digit IDs)
Migration path defined if legacy IDs exist
Constants must point to actual files:
"""Verify all path constants point to existing files."""
Every specified feature must have working implementation:
Implements identity resolution per spec in identity_resolution.yaml.
Never ignore input - always validate and use:
# Bug: hardcoded instead of reading metrics_file
raise ValueError("Need at least 2 runs for determinism check")
"""Verify function behavior changes with different inputs."""
assert score_good != score_bad, "Function not using input!"
Test reports must come from actual test runs:
Audit all commands for platform compatibility:
'grep', # Use Python: [x for x in items if pattern in x]
'grep "pattern" file': 'python: [line for line in open("file") if "pattern" in line]',
- [ ] External references to migrated files updated
- [ ] No orphan files (all have bundle membership)
- [ ] All new paths added to registry
- [ ] All files accessible at new paths
description: Update schemas, constants, path structure
description: Move files, update IDs, adapt imports
description: Update registry with new paths and IDs
description: Remove old files, delete legacy artifacts
Include this validation script in every plan:
Comprehensive plan validation - run before execution.
FAIL FAST if any check fails.
if section in self.plan and not self.plan[section]:
f"ID at {field_path} is {type(id_value)}, must be string"
f"ID at {field_path} is '{id_value}', must be 20 digits"
# Check for timestamp in ID generation
if 'time.time()' in id_gen or 'datetime.now()' in id_gen:
if 'hashing' in self.plan or 'id_generation' in self.plan:
"Hashing/ID generation present but no canonicalization protocol defined"
missing = [k for k in required if k not in protocol]
# Check for labels in ID fields
f"Label '{id_value}' in ID field {field_path} - use 20-digit canonical ID"
# Must explicitly mark SSOT vs derived
if 'ssot_layer' not in schema and 'derived_from' not in schema:
f"Entity '{entity}' not marked as SSOT or derived"
# Derived fields must be marked readOnly
# Must have both PLANNED and PRESENT
missing = [k for k in required if k not in baseline]
"Rollback plan present but no backup_evidence defined"
# All examples must include required args
# Check for procedural rules in registry
missing = [step for step in required_steps if step not in migration]
"""Find all values for a given key."""
Before delivering ANY implementation plan, AI must verify:
No contradictory definitions (same concept defined multiple ways)
All structural elements present (no empty placeholders)
Single canonical ID scheme (no mixed formats)
No timestamps in IDs or content hashes
Array ordering specified (semantic sort key documented)
All canonical IDs are exactly 20 digits
All IDs stored as strings (never numeric types)
ID validation functions include leading-zero checks
ID type codes match entity types
Data model layers documented (SSOT vs derived)
Rebuild procedures specified for derived data
No manual edits to derived fields
All merge scenarios have explicit policies
Lock timeout and staleness handling specified
Test reports generated by real tools (not fabricated)
Strict mode is default (not weak mode)
Registry contains facts only (no procedural rules)
AI heuristics validated before becoming SSOT
All inputs validated and used (not ignored)
Test artifacts generated by real tools
1. Load this document first - Review all categories before planning
2. Run structural checks - Verify no contradictions, missing elements, or ID conflicts
3. Apply determinism rules - Specify canonicalization for all ID/hash generation
4. Enforce ID integrity - All canonical IDs must be 20-digit strings with validation
5. Maintain namespace discipline - No labels/aliases in canonical ID fields
6. Respect SSOT boundaries - Document layers, read from SSOT, rebuild derived
7. Separate intent from reality - Use PLANNED/PRESENT states, two-phase
8. Implement concurrency control - CAS baselines, drift detection, single-writer
9. Require evidence - Post-apply validation, backup/restore, rollback capability
10. Validate reporting - Consistent metrics, verifiable claims, real test artifacts
11. Complete migrations fully - All 12 steps (M1-M12), tested rollback, phased
12. Run validation script - Include and execute comprehensive validation before
13. Check pre-delivery checklist - All items must pass
14. Fail fast - If any validation fails, ABORT and fix before proceeding
CRITICAL: This is not optional guidance. Every rule in this document prevents a real,
observed failure mode. Treat this as a compilation firewall - plans that violate these
